name: Run Bash
description: Execute a bash script in a sandboxed Docker container.

function.name: bash
function.description: Execute a bash script in an isolated Docker container. The container is shared with the runpy tool within the same session — files written by Python code are immediately accessible to bash scripts and vice versa. The working directory is /workspace, which is the project root. The container persists across calls so state (environment variables set with export, files in /tmp, installed packages) survives between script executions within the same session.

script.description: The bash script to execute. Can be multi-line. Runs via 'bash -s' (stdin). The working directory is /workspace.

action.description: Action to perform. 'run' (default) executes the script. 'destroy' tears down the container for this session and frees resources (also destroys the shared runpy container).

timeout.description: Maximum execution time in seconds. Default 60, max 300.

env.description: Optional environment variables injected for this execution only. Keys and values must be strings.
